title: erica coccinea subsp. coccinea
intro: Erica coccinea subsp. coccinea, also known as the Scottish heather or bell heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Scotland and Ireland. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Erica coccinea subsp. coccinea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antioxidant. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, soothe digestive issues, and support immune function.
